title: Dynamic programming - solve it once, remember it
author: Prodipta Acharjee
concept: When a problem keeps re-solving the same subproblems, store each answer the first time (memoize) so you compute it once - turning exponential work into linear.
explanation: Naive recursive fibonacci is a trap: fib(n) calls fib(n-1) and fib(n-2), which both recompute the same smaller values, again and again, exploding to O(2^n). But there are only n distinct answers. Dynamic programming is the simple fix: keep a cache, and before computing fib(n), check if you already have it. Each value is worked out once, so it collapses to O(n). The whole idea is overlapping subproblems plus a memory.

exercises.question: What does this print?
hints: fib(10) is the eleventh term (counting fib(0)=0).

exercises.question: Dynamic programming speeds things up by...
hints: The cache is the heart of it.
Don't solve the same small problem twice.

exercises.question: Memoizing fibonacci changes it from exponential to...
hints: There are only n distinct values to compute.
